_disclaimer: APPROXIMATIONS ONLY — NOT LSAC EQUATING. Sources: Magoosh LSAT blog (approx. 55 PrepTests analyzed, 2022-2024); PowerScore LSAT Logical Reasoning Bible (2024-25 ed.); LSAC PrepTest score conversion charts (publicly available). These weights are suitable for estimating exam-frequency-weighted readiness projections; they are NOT the official equating algorithm. Cite clearly in any UI that shows a Readiness score.

lr_frequency_weights._description: Approximate frequency of each LR question type as a fraction of all scored LR items. Sums to 1.0 over the 13-type taxonomy (D-SR13). Based on Magoosh analysis of ~55 PrepTests (cited source). 'Flaw most common; Assumption+Flaw+Inference ~40%; +Strengthen/Weaken/Paradox/Principle ~75%' (brainlift J.1).
lr_frequency_weights._source: Magoosh LSAT blog, 'LSAT Question Type Frequencies' (2022 update); PowerScore LRB 2024-25; cross-checked against Cambridge LSAT frequency data.
lr_frequency_weights._comment_on_form_count: Post-2024 LSAT has 2 scored LR sections of ~24-26 questions each = ~50 LR items per form. RC contributes ~26-28 items. Total form = ~76 items.

items_per_form_by_type._description: Expected count of each question type on a typical post-2024 LSAT form (LR only, ~50 items). Derived as round(w_S * 50). Fractional values mean the count varies by ~1 across forms.

readiness_formula_note.clarification: The spec lists w_S twice in the formula; interpretation here is that w_S enters only through items_per_skill_on_form(S) = w_S * N_lr. At Perf=1 for all covered skills, expected_lr_raw = N_lr = 50. See L5 in WP-1-log.md for the ambiguity detail.
readiness_formula_note.v1_coverage_note: v1 covers LR only. expected_lr_raw is a partial estimate. To project to scaled score, we estimate total_raw = expected_lr_raw + rc_raw_assumed, where rc_raw_assumed is imputed (see raw_to_scaled_conversion._lr_only_projection_note).
projection_options: Option A (conservative): assume RC Perf = mean LR Perf. total_raw = expected_lr_raw / 0.66 (LR ~66% of form).
Option B (honest): report LR-only Readiness labeled 'LR-only projected score'. Treat the LR portion as 66% of the exam.
Option C (deferred): abstain on Readiness until RC coverage added in phase-2.
readiness_formula_note.recommended_v1: Option B — report LR-only with an honest label. Ambiguity logged as L5.

raw_to_scaled_conversion._disclaimer: APPROXIMATION. Derived from publicly available LSAC PrepTest score conversion charts (PrepTests 101-158, post-2024 format). True equating is LSAC-proprietary and form-specific; this table is a representative approximation suitable for orientation only.
raw_to_scaled_conversion._source: LSAC PrepTest 101-120 conversion charts (public release); supplemented by 7Sage LSAT score conversion data (2024).
raw_to_scaled_conversion._comment: Maps total form raw score (LR + RC, ~76 items) to scaled 120-180. For LR-only projection see readiness_formula_note.
raw_to_scaled_conversion._lr_only_projection_note: To map LR-only expected_raw to scaled: estimate total_raw = expected_lr_raw + (lr_pct_of_form_assumed * N_total / lr_pct_of_form_assumed). Simplest: total_raw_estimate = expected_lr_raw * (76/50). Then look up in this table. Label output 'LR-only estimate ±wide-band'.
